title: Bookmark Slice Transition
prompt:
Create a production-ready Bookmark Slice Transition using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original transition swaps a short reading summary with a saved-note panel by sliding one narrow bookmark slice across the content before the next panel appears. Use a white 22px Bento panel on #F4F5F6, #ECECEE border, subtle shadow, #111111 headings, and #4A4A4F body copy. Use one lemon bookmark accent and no card gradient, full-screen overlay, or automatic looping.

Provide one semantic 44px toggle button labelled Show saved note or Show summary, with aria-expanded and a polite live status. Click, Enter, and Space must reverse the panel state. Animate the bookmark slice with a spring around stiffness 280 damping 28, then fade and translate the panel 10px over 180ms ease-out. Use AnimatePresence where helpful; keep local state only and prefer transform and opacity. Do not use timers, random values, browser storage, network calls, remote assets, or page-level fixed positioning.

The interaction must make sense inside a compact preview at 320px and grow naturally for a larger detail view. Keep a visible dark focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and labels that make both states understandable without motion or color. Under prefers-reduced-motion, replace the slice travel with an immediate panel swap and a short fade. Return complete client component code and a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:

Summary

*/ .bookmark-slice{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.bookmark-slice p{padding:16px;border-left:12px solid #f5e642;border-radius:16px;background:#f4f5f6;color:#4a4a4f}.bookmark-slice button{min-height:44px;border:1px solid #111;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.bookmark-slice button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.bookmark-slice *{transition:none}}
